perturbation.original_text: All fees due to Capital Resources but unpaid will be payable to Capital Resources in next day funds at the earlier of the Closing Date (as hereinafter defined) or the End Date. In the event the Subscription and Public Offerings are extended beyond the End Date, the Company, the -2- Association and Capital Resources may mutually agree to renew this Agreement under mutually acceptable terms.
perturbation.changed_text: All fees due to Capital Resources but unpaid will be payable to Capital Resources in next day funds at the later of the Closing Date (as hereinafter defined) or the End Date. In the event the Subscription and Public Offerings are extended beyond the End Date, the Company, the -2- Association and Capital Resources may mutually agree to renew this Agreement under mutually acceptable terms.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing 'earlier' to 'later'. This contradicts the intention of ensuring prompt payment by deferring payment until the later of two dates, while the original agreement intended payment by the earlier date. This creates uncertainty as the contract now has two conflicting clauses regarding when the payment is due and introduces legal uncertainty and potential disputes over obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The closing for the sale of the Shares shall take place on the Closing Date at the offices of Capital Resources or such other location as mutually agreed upon by Capital Resources, the Company and the Association. At the closing, the Association shall deliver to Capital Resources in next day funds the commissions, fees and expenses due and owing to Capital Resources as set forth in Sections 2 and 7 hereof and the opinions and certificates required hereby and other documents deemed reasonably necessary by Capital Resources shall be executed and delivered to effect the sale of the Shares as contemplated hereby and pursuant to the terms of the Offering Prospectus.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment due date from the 'earlier' to the 'later' of the Closing Date or End Date. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that commissions, fees, and expenses are due and owing to Capital Resources in next day funds 'at the closing', implying an earlier payment obligation. The change introduces a clear inconsistency regarding when Capital Resources is entitled to payment, potentially leading to a dispute over contractual obligations. As the 'earlier' and 'later' cause conflicting deadlines for when payment should be made, that creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Capital Resources shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: (a) (i) a marketing fee in the amount of (x) two percent (2.0%) of the aggregate dollar amount of all Shares sold in the Subscription and Public Offerings, excluding sales made through broker assisted purchases or by other NASD member firms participating in the Subscription and Public Offerings pursuant to the Selected Dealers' Agreement, if any (for which Capital Resources' compensation shall be pursuant to sub-paragraph (ii)) and excluding shares sold to the Association's Employee Stock Ownership Plan, directors, officers or employees and any member of such person's immediate family (defined to include children, spouse, parents, grandparents and grandchildren); (ii) a management fee in the amount of one percent and one-half (1.5%) of the aggregate dollar amount of Shares sold through broker assisted purchases or through selected dealers, if any.
perturbation.changed_text: Capital Resources shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: (a) (i) a marketing fee in the amount of (x) two percent (2.0%) of the aggregate dollar amount of all Shares sold in the Subscription and Public Offerings, including sales made through broker assisted purchases or by other NASD member firms participating in the Subscription and Public Offerings pursuant to the Selected Dealers' Agreement, if any (for which Capital Resources' compensation shall be pursuant to sub-paragraph (ii)) and excluding shares sold to the Association's Employee Stock Ownership Plan, directors, officers or employees and any member of such person's immediate family (defined to include children, spouse, parents, grandparents and grandchildren); (ii) a management fee in the amount of three percent (3.0%) of the aggregate dollar amount of Shares sold through broker assisted purchases or through selected dealers, if any.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing 'one percent and one-half (1.5%)' to 'three percent (3.0%)'. This creates uncertainty as the contract now has two conflicting clauses regarding compensation, potentially leading to disputes over the correct fee and introduces legal uncertainty and potential disputes over obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The closing for the sale of the Shares shall take place on the Closing Date at the offices of Capital Resources or such other location as mutually agreed upon by Capital Resources, the Company and the Association. At the closing, the Association shall deliver to Capital Resources in next day funds the commissions, fees and expenses due and owing to Capital Resources as set forth in Sections 2 and 7 hereof and the opinions and certificates required hereby and other documents deemed reasonably necessary by Capital Resources shall be executed and delivered to effect the sale of the Shares as contemplated hereby and pursuant to the terms of the Offering Prospectus.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the management fee from 1.5% to 3.0%. The contradicted text states that at closing, Capital Resources shall receive the fees as set forth in Sections 2 and 7. Section 2, as modified, now contains contradictory information regarding the management fee, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the amount due to Capital Resources, thus making enforcement unclear. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'. In this instance, there are conflicting obligations regarding fees, specifically the management fee due to Capital Resources.

perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Association will use the net proceeds from the sale of the Shares in the manner set forth in the Offering Prospectus under the caption "Use of Proceeds."
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Association will use the net proceeds from the sale of the Shares at their own discretion, without any constraints.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by allowing the Company and the Association to use the net proceeds at their own discretion, contrasting with the original clause that stated proceeds must be used in the manner described in the "Use of Proceeds" section of the Offering Prospectus. This contradiction removes the obligation to adhere to the outlined purpose, causing uncertainty regarding the intended use and introduces legal uncertainty and potential disputes over obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (o) The Company and the Association will use the net proceeds from the sale of the Shares in the manner set forth in the Offering Prospectus under the caption "Use of Proceeds."
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly grants the Company and the Association the discretion to use the proceeds from the sale of Shares "at their own discretion, without any constraints," which directly contradicts the contradicted text stating they "will use the net proceeds from the sale of the Shares in the manner set forth in the Offering Prospectus under the caption 'Use of Proceeds.'" This creates an in-text contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it provides conflicting obligations concerning the use of proceeds.
